rules:
Discard 2 cards from your hand. If you do, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

After counting your Prize cards, shuffle them into your deck. Then, take that many cards from the top of your deck and put them face down as your Prize cards.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Gumshoe Chance-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mad Bull-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sunsteel Strike
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sol Burst-GX
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 5 Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Strafe
attacks.effect: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadow Bullet
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Dark Call-GX
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from your opponent's Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Moongeist Beam
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't be healed during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Lunar Fall-GX
attacks.effect: Knock Out 1 of your opponent's Basic Pokémon that isn't a Pokémon-GX. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage times the number of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Divide-GX
attacks.effect: Put 10 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Blizzard Burn
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

attacks.name: Ice Beam-GX
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Flower Supply
attacks.effect: Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Solar Blade
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Chloroscythe-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage times the amount of Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Your opponent reveals their hand. Discard 2 Energy cards from it.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Draw 2 cards. During this turn, your Pokémon's attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand. If it's your first turn, draw cards until you have 8 cards in your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Each player shuffles their hand into their deck and flips a coin. If heads, that player draws 6 cards. If tails, they draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Gumshoe Chance-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mad Bull-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sol Burst-GX
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 5 Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.effect: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from your opponent's Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't be healed during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.effect: Knock Out 1 of your opponent's Basic Pokémon that isn't a Pokémon-GX. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.effect: Put 10 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.effect: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Blizzard Burn
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

attacks.name: Ice Beam-GX
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Flower Supply
attacks.effect: Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Solar Blade
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Chloroscythe-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage times the amount of Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

This card provides Colorless Energy. While in play, this card provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. When you attach this card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon.
Double Colorless Energy provides ColorlessColorless Energy.

Discard 2 cards from your hand. If you do, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Flip 2 coins. For each heads, search your deck for an Evolution Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Your opponent reveals their hand. Discard 2 Energy cards from it.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

After counting your Prize cards, shuffle them into your deck. Then, take that many cards from the top of your deck and put them face down as your Prize cards.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it. You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Draw 2 cards. During this turn, your Pokémon's attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

If the Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand. If it's your first turn, draw cards until you have 8 cards in your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Each player shuffles their hand into their deck and flips a coin. If heads, that player draws 6 cards. If tails, they draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

When your Active Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, you may move 1 basic Energy card from that Pokémon to the Pokémon this card is attached to.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Move a basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Put 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Heal 20 damage and remove a Special Condition from your Active Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Deep in the jungle, high in the lofty canopy, this Pokémon abides. On rare occasions, it shows up at the beach to match wits with Slowking.

attacks.name: Bear Hug
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Superpower
attacks.effect: You may do 40 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: This immensely dangerous Pokémon possesses overwhelming physical strength. Its habitat is generally off-limits.
Despite its adorable appearance, when it gets angry and flails about, its arms and legs could knock a pro wrestler sprawling.

attacks.name: Gumshoe Chance-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: With its sharp fangs, it will bite anything. It did not originally live in Alola but was imported from another region.

attacks.name: Echoed Voice
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Echoed Voice attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Beak Blast
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Within its beak, its internal gas ignites, explosively launching seeds with enough power to pulverize boulders.

attacks.name: Bullet Seed
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: By bending its beak, it can produce a variety of call and brand itself a noisy nuisance for its neighbors.

attacks.name: Rock Smash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon feeds on berries, whose leftover seeds become the ammunition for the attacks it fires off from its mouth.

attacks.name: Ferocious Bellow
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 50 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Intelligent, good-natured, and valiant, it's a trustworthy partner on rescue teams.
Its dense black fur grows continuously. The high cost of keeping its hard fur properly groomed make this a troublesome Pokémon to train.

attacks.name: Work Up
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Because it doesn't yelp, it's extremely popular with Trainers who live in apartment buildings.

attacks.name: Teeter Punch
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its steps are staggering and unsteady, but Spinda thinks it's walking in a straight line.

attacks.name: Quick Draw
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, draw a card.

flavorText: Possessing an unbalanced and unstable genetic makeup, it conceals many possible evolutions.

attacks.name: Quick Draw
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, draw a card.

flavorText: Possessing an unbalanced and unstable genetic makeup, it conceals many possible evolutions.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mad Bull-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Cross-Cut
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Hurricane Punch
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Kangaskhan's maternal love is so deep that it will brave death to protect its offspring.

attacks.name: Devastating Wind
attacks.effect: Your opponent shuffles their hand into their deck and draws 3 cards.

attacks.name: Slashing Strike
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't use Slashing Strike during your next turn.

flavorText: Drawings of a Pokémon resembling Fearow can be seen in murals from deep in ancient history.

attacks.name: Peck Bugs
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Grass Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Farmers whose fields are troubled by bug Pokémon appreciate Spearow for its vigorous appetite and look after it.

attacks.name: Dragon Wave
attacks.effect: Discard a Grass Energy and a Lightning Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Giant Tail
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Incur the wrath of this normally calm Pokémon at your peril, because it will smash everything to smithereens before it's satisfied.

attacks.name: Dragon's Wish
attacks.effect: During your next turn, you may attach any number of Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon.

flavorText: From time immemorial, it has been venerated by agricultural peoples as an entity able to control the weather.

attacks.name: Signs of Evolution
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Dratini, a Dragonair, and a Dragonite, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: After a 10-hour struggle, a fisherman was able to pull one up and confirm its existence.
It rolls up pollen into puffs. It makes many different varieties, some used as food and others used in battle.

attacks.name: Fly Around
attacks.effect: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.

flavorText: It feeds on the nectar and pollen of flowers. Because it's able to sense auras, it can identify which flowers are about to bloom.

attacks.name: Dark Clamp
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Fight Back
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: More timid than Snubbull, this Pokémon is doted on by young people amused at the contrast between its looks and its attitude.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its growls make its opponents uneasy. This laid-back Pokémon tends to sleep half the day.

attacks.name: Sunsteel Strike
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sol Burst-GX
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 5 Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Metallic Sound
attacks.effect: Discard all Special Energy from each Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aerial Ace
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Its feathers, which fall off as it grows, are thin and sharp. In times long past, warriors used them as swords.

attacks.name: Dig Under
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: Its shining gold hair provides it with protection. It's reputed that keeping any of its fallen hairs will bring bad luck.

attacks.name: Spelunk
attacks.effect: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck and put them back in any order.

flavorText: Its head sports an altered form of whiskers made of metal. When in communication with its comrades, its whiskers wobble to and fro.

attacks.name: False Accusation
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each card in your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Obsidian Fang
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tool cards from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its unique faculty of sight can detect small prey more than 30 miles away, even in the midst of a sandstorm.

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.effect: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: They move in groups of a few individuals. A female is often the leader of the group, and the males will gather food.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It submerges itself in sand and moves as if swimming. This wise behavior keeps its enemies from finding it and maintains its temperature.

attacks.name: Aqua Impact
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: It has a sad history. In the past, its dorsal fin was a treasured foodstuff, so this Pokémon became a victim of overfishing.

attacks.name: Gnaw Through
attacks.effect: Discard all Pokémon Tool cards from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: If they scent the faintest trace of blood, they rush to attack en masse. When alone, they're rather cowardly.

attacks.name: Strafe
attacks.effect: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadow Bullet
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Dark Call-GX
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from your opponent's Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Taunt
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Claw Rend
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Its round face and smooth coat—softer than the most high-class velvet—have made this a very popular Pokémon in Alola.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: This Pokémon was not originally found in Alola. Human actions caused a surge in their numbers, and they went feral. They're prideful and crafty.

attacks.name: Evil Orders
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a number of cards up to the number of your Benched Pokémon and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Endeavor
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: This gourmet Pokémon is particular about the taste and freshness of its food. Restaurants where Raticate live have a good reputation.
When the sun goes down, it becomes active. It runs around town on a chase for good food for the boss of its nest—Raticate.

attacks.name: Absorb Vitality
attacks.effect: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Buried beneath the castle are masses of dried-up bones from those whose vitality it has drained.

attacks.name: Shore Up
attacks.effect: Attach a Fighting Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sand Tomb
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It takes control of anyone who puts a hand in its mouth. And so it adds to the accumulation of its sand-mound body.

attacks.name: Fling
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Team Play
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched Passimian.

flavorText: They form groups of roughly 20 individuals. Their mutual bond is remarkable—they will never let down a comrade.
It punches so much, its pincers often come off from overuse, but they grow back quickly. What little meat they contain is rich and delicious.

attacks.name: Rock Artillery
attacks.effect: Discard any amount of Fighting Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Known for its hefty horsepower, this Pokémon is a popular partner for construction workers.

attacks.name: Rock Hurl
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It explores caves in search of underground water. It's not comfortable around water, so this Pokémon takes great care in lapping it up.

attacks.name: Smack Down
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has Fighting Resistance, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: The hexagonal cavity is its ear. It walks in the direction of sounds it hears, but if the sounds cease, it panics and topples over.

attacks.name: Push Out
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It is known for its fantastic strength, but as it grows older, it focuses more on training Makuhita.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It was originally brought in from another region, but now Makuhita from Alola are more famous.

attacks.name: Moongeist Beam
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't be healed during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Lunar Fall-GX
attacks.effect: Knock Out 1 of your opponent's Basic Pokémon that isn't a Pokémon-GX. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Teleport
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Motionless as if dead, its body is faintly warm to the touch. In the distant past, it was called the cocoon of the stars.

attacks.name: Dust Gathering
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: Its body is gaseous and frail. It slowly grows as it collects dust from the atmosphere.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: Toxapex crawls along the ocean floor on its 12 legs. It leaves a trail of Corsola bits scattered in its wake.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It plunges the poison spike on its head into its prey. When the prey has weakened, Mareanie deals the finishing blow with its 10 tentacles.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Divide-GX
attacks.effect: Put 10 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Meditate
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: As a matter of course, it makes anyone it meets fall asleep and has a taste of their dreams. Anyone having a good dream, it carries off.

attacks.name: Psychic Boom
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It finds really fun dreams tasty. When it makes friends with people, it may show them the most delicious dreams it's ever eaten.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: While it's unexpectedly quiet and friendly, if it's not fed any trash for a while, it will smash its Trainer's furnishings and eat up the fragments.

attacks.name: Super Poison Breath
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The crystals on Grimer's body are lumps of toxins. If one falls off, lethal poisons leak out.

attacks.name: Triple Poison
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 3 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Surprise Strike
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon was on the Bench and became your Active Pokémon this turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Both its legs became wings, and as a result, it can't move well on the ground. All it can do is crawl around.

attacks.name: Super Poison Breath
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Acrobatics
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its thick fangs are hollow like straws, making them unexpectedly fragile. These fangs are specialized for sucking blood.

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.effect: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

flavorText: It sleeps in caves during the day. It has no eyes, so to check its surroundings while flying, it emits ultrasonic waves.

attacks.name: Defense Curl
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Discharge
attacks.effect: Discard all Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: The long hairs on its back act as lightning rods. The bolts of lightning it attracts are stored as energy in its electric sac.

attacks.name: Electro Cannon
attacks.effect: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It produces electricity via an electrical organ in its abdomen. It overwhelms bird Pokémon with shocking beams of electrical energy.

attacks.name: Shocking Jaws
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: From the food it digests, it generates electricity, and it stores this energy in its electric sac.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Aqua Spark
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Water Energy attached to it, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon flashes a bright light that blinds its prey. This creates an opening for it to deliver an electrical attack.

attacks.name: Searching Light
attacks.effect: Look at 1 of your face-down Prize cards.

flavorText: It lives in the depths beyond the reach of sunlight. It flashes lights on its antennae to communicate with others of its kind.

attacks.name: Vivid Charge
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Psychic Fangs
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It stuns its prey with psychokinesis and then grinds them to mush with its strong teeth. Even Shellder's shell is no match for it.

attacks.name: Continuous Tumble
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It lives in shallow seas, such as areas near a beach. It can eject its internal organs, which it uses to engulf its prey or battle enemies.
It delivers headbutts with the water bubble on its head. Small Pokémon get sucked into the bubble, where they drown.

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It crawls onto the land in search of food. Its water bubble allows it to breathe and protects its soft head.
When it's in trouble, its eyes moisten and begin to shine. The shining light attracts its comrades, and they stand together against their enemies.

attacks.name: Avalanche
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It just throws punches indiscriminately. In times of desperation, it can lop off its own pincers and fire them like rockets.

attacks.name: Bubble Beat
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Water Energy attached to your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Roaring Seas
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Grand Echo-GX
attacks.effect: Heal all damage from all of your Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Disarming Voice
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Sparkling Aria
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It controls its water balloons with song. The melody is learning from others of its kind and is passed down from one generation to the next.

attacks.name: Disarming Voice
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: A skillful dancer, it creates a sequence of water balloons as it dances, and briskly bombards it enemies.
This Pokémon snorts body fluids from its nose, blowing balloons to smash into its foes. It's famous for being a hard worker.

attacks.name: Courier
attacks.effect: Put 1 of your Benched Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your hand.

attacks.name: Fly
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Gathering food is the work of young males. They store food in their capacious beaks and carry it back to others waiting in the nest.

attacks.name: Roost
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon. It can't retreat during your next turn.

flavorText: Fishermen keep an eye out for Wingull in the sky, because wherever they're circling, the ocean sure to be is teeming with fish Pokémon.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Pursued by Mareanie for the branches on its head, this Pokémon will sometimes snap its own branches off as a diversion while it escapes.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Blizzard Burn
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

attacks.name: Ice Beam-GX
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Headlock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its hard shell cannot be shattered—not even by a bomb. The contents of the shell remain unknown.
The hardness of its shell surpasses the hardness of a diamond. In days gone by, people used the shells to make shields.

attacks.name: Split Spiral Punch
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Wake-Up Slap
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 80 more damage. Then, remove all Special Conditions from that Pokémon.

flavorText: Its percentage of body fat is nearly zero. Its body is entirely muscle, which makes it heavy and forces its swimming prowess to develop.

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It marches over the land in search of bug Pokémon to eat. Then it takes them underwater so it can dine on them where it's safe.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage times the amount of Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It's still not very good at walking. Its Trainers should train this Pokémon to walk every day.

attacks.name: Double Jet
attacks.effect: Discard up to 2 Water Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: It is said that the red part of its forehead grants supernatural powers to those who posses one, so it was over-hunted in the past.

attacks.name: Confusion Wave
attacks.effect: Both Active Pokémon are now Confused.

flavorText: As a result of headaches so fierce they cause it to cry, it sometimes uses psychokinesis without meaning to.

attacks.name: Hustling Strike
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Fire Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tiger Swing
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 more damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Burning Slam-GX
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fire Fang
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Darkest Lariat
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.

flavorText: This Pokémon has a violent, selfish disposition. If it's not in the mood to listen, it will ignore its Trainer's orders with complete nonchalance.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: At its throat, it bears a bell of fire. The bell rings brightly whenever this Pokémon spits fire.
While grooming itself, it builds up fur inside its stomach. It sets the fur alight and spews fiery attacks, which change based on how it coughs.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Coal is the source of Torkoal's energy. Large amounts of coal can be found in the mounts where they live.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Firestorm
attacks.effect: Discard 3 Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Overflowing with beauty and majesty, this strong Pokémon appears in ancient Eastern folklore.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It's both clever and loyal, but if a stranger tries to invade its territory, it barks threateningly.

attacks.name: Trop Kick
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage and remove all Special Conditions from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its long, striking legs aren't just for show but to be used to kick with skill. In victory, it shows off by kicking the defeated, laughing boisterously.

attacks.name: Sweet Scent
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: The sepals on its head developed to protect its body. These are quite hard, so even if pecked by bird Pokémon, this Pokémon is totally fine.

attacks.name: Sweet Scent
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: A delectable aroma pours from its body. They are often swallowed whole by Toucannon lured by that wafting deliciousness.

attacks.name: Flickering Spores
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It emits flickering spores that cause drowsiness. When its prey succumb to sleep, this Pokémon feeds on them by sucking in their energy.

attacks.name: Flickering Spores
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: As it drowses the day away, it nourishes itself by sucking from tree roots. It wakens at the fall of night, wandering off in search of a new tree.

attacks.name: Flower Supply
attacks.effect: Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Solar Blade
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Chloroscythe-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage times the amount of Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Synthesis
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Grass Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: They give off a sweet and refreshing scent. Cutiefly often gather near the tall grass where Fomantis are hiding.
They often gather near places frequented by electric Pokémon in order to avoid being attacked by bird Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hollow Hunt-GX
attacks.effect: Put 3 cards from your discard pile into your hand. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Brave Bird
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It fires arrow quills from its wings with such precision, they can pierce a pebble at distances over a hundred yards.

attacks.name: Sharp Blade Quill
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: A bit of a dandy, it spends its free time preening its wings. Its preoccupation with any dirt on its plumage can leave it unable to battle.
This wary Pokémon uses photosynthesis to store up energy during the day, while becoming active at night.

attacks.name: Struggle Bug
attacks.effect: Move an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon to 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its wings and antennae don't cope well with moisture. After a rain, it faces sunward to dry off.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: When this Pokémon senses danger, a sweet fluid oozes from the tip of its heads. The taste of it disgusts bird Pokémon.

attacks.name: Roof Fling
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, put your opponent's Active Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your opponent's hand.

flavorText: One solid blow from its horns is enough to split apart a large tree. Its greatest rival in Alola is Vikavolt.

attacks.name: Fury Cutter
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, this attack does 20 more damage. If 2 of them are heads, this attack does 60 more damage. If all of them are heads, this attack does 120 damage.

attacks.name: Mushroom Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The large mushroom on its back controls it. It often fights over territory with Shiinotic.
No matter how much it eats, the mushrooms growing on its back steal away most of the nutrients it consumes.

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Close examination of its large eyes reveals that each eye is composed of a myriad of tiny eyes.

attacks.name: Iron Defense
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Its shell is filled with its soft innards. It doesn't move much because of the risk it might carelessly spill its innards out.

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When attacked by bird Pokémon, it resists by releasing a terrifically strong odor from its antennae, but it often becomes their prey.
